quote_text: We have reached the heights if we know what it is that we find joy in.
reflection: What does your joy reveal about what you value?

quote_text: I am not a hindrance to myself.
reflection: What fear loses power when you refuse to obstruct yourself?

quote_text: Those who seem to be busied with nothing are busied with the greater tasks.
reflection: What quiet work may serve others more than visible busyness?

quote_text: If you would make any thing a habit, do it.
reflection: What small act of restraint can begin the larger discipline?

quote_text: A man cannot lose either the past or the future.
reflection: What worry can you release because it is not actually in your hands?

quote_text: The spirit that will never consent to come under the jurisdiction of things external to ourselves.
reflection: Where can you refuse the rule of circumstances without denying them?

quote_text: As for justice, would not the woman who studies philosophy be just, would she not be a blameless life-partner?
reflection: How can justice show up in the closest partnership you have?

quote_text: Never say about any thing, I have lost it, but say I have restored it.
reflection: What can you hold more lightly by remembering it was never owned?

quote_text: Whatever years lie behind us are in death's hands.
reflection: How can remembering spent time sharpen your use of this day?

quote_text: Consider these things, if you would exchange for them, freedom from passions, liberty, tranquillity.
reflection: What price are you willing to pay for steadier freedom?

quote_text: The woman trained in philosophy would be disposed to look upon doing a wrong as worse than suffering one.
reflection: Where would you rather suffer wrong than commit it?

quote_text: Be satisfied then in every thing with being a philosopher.
reflection: What would be enough today if your aim were simply to live wisely?

quote_text: Philosophy is no trick to catch the public; it is not devised for show.
reflection: Where can you choose substance over looking wise?

quote_text: Even bad fortune is fickle. Perhaps it will come, perhaps not; in the meantime it is not.
reflection: What possible trouble can you stop treating as present?

quote_text: Do not however be disagreeable to those who indulge in these pleasures, or reprove them.
reflection: How can your restraint remain gentle toward people who choose differently?

quote_text: The measure of possession is to every man the body, as the foot is of the shoe.
reflection: What possession has grown beyond the measure of real need?

quote_text: If you look for it in a different place from where it is, what wonder if you never find it?
reflection: What are you seeking in the wrong place?

quote_text: Perhaps it happens for my best interests; it may be that such a death will shed credit upon my life.
reflection: What hardship can you meet as material for honor?

quote_text: But both courses are to be avoided; you should not copy the bad simply because they are many, nor should you hate the many because they are unlike you.
reflection: Where can you refuse the crowd without contempt for the crowd?

quote_text: On no occasion call yourself a philosopher, and do not speak much among the uninstructed about theorems.
reflection: Where can quiet practice replace announcing your principles?

quote_text: It is more important for you to keep the resolutions you have already made than to go on and make noble ones.
reflection: Which old resolution needs keeping more than you need a new one?

quote_text: Death certainly, and life, honor and dishonor, pain and pleasure, all these things equally happen to good men and bad.
reflection: What outside condition can you stop treating as proof of your worth?

quote_text: The true friendship which hope and fear and self-interest cannot sever.
reflection: Which friendship deserves action free from self-interest?

quote_text: When you are eating with another, remember to look not only to the value for the body of the things set before you.
reflection: How can consideration for another person shape your appetite?

quote_text: Some things torment us more than they ought; some torment us before they ought.
reflection: Which torment can you reduce by meeting it at its true size?

quote_text: He will be satisfied with what is left, taking as much pleasure in his impaired and maimed body as he took when it was sound.
reflection: What loss could you meet by honoring what remains?

quote_text: He who regards himself only, and enters upon friendships for this reason, reckons wrongly.
reflection: Where can you make friendship less about your own advantage?

quote_text: Wish to be considered to know nothing.
reflection: Where would humility free you from performing certainty?

quote_text: If fear wins a majority of the votes, incline in the other direction anyhow, and cease to harass your soul.
reflection: What decision needs less harassment and more clear judgment?

quote_text: The outcome of every fight is death, and the means are fire and sword.
reflection: What spectacle or conflict should you refuse because it trains cruelty?

quote_text: Not one then of the bad lives as he wishes; nor is he then free.
reflection: What ungoverned habit is making you less free than you want to be?
